GitHub.description: Configuration for GitHub Apps, OAuth and the token broker used by Costellobot.

AccessToken.description: A personal access token used as a fallback for authenticating with GitHub.

Apps.description: A dictionary of GitHub App configurations, keyed by App ID.

BadgesKey.description: A secret key used to sign/validate requests for status badges.

ClientId.description: The OAuth client ID used for sign-in.

ClientSecret.description: The OAuth client secret used for sign-in.

EnterpriseDomain.description: The domain name of the GitHub Enterprise instance, if applicable.

Installations.description: A dictionary of GitHub App installation configurations, keyed by Installation ID.

OAuthId.description: An identifier used for OAuth-related configuration.

Scopes.description: The OAuth scopes requested for user sign-in.

GitHubAppOptions.description: Configuration for a single GitHub App registration.

AppId.description: The numeric ID of the GitHub App, as a string.

ClientId.description: The OAuth client ID associated with the GitHub App.

HasWebhook.description: Whether the GitHub App is configured with a webhook that Costellobot should process events from.

Name.description: The display name (slug) of the GitHub App.

Organization.description: The GitHub organization that the App belongs to, if applicable.

PrivateKey.description: The PEM-encoded private key used to authenticate as the GitHub App.

GitHubInstallationOptions.description: Maps a GitHub App installation ID to the App that it belongs to.

AppId.description: The App ID (key into GitHub:Apps) that this installation corresponds to.

Organization.description: The GitHub organization the installation belongs to, if applicable.

GitHubOpenIdConnectOptions.description: Configuration for validating OpenID Connect tokens (e.g. from GitHub Actions).

Audiences.description: The accepted audience values for OIDC tokens.

Issuers.description: The accepted issuer values for OIDC tokens.

MetadataUri.description: The URI of the OIDC discovery document used to validate tokens.

GitHubTokenBrokerOptions.description: Configuration for the GitHub access token broker.

IsEnabled.description: Whether the token broker functionality is enabled.

Owner.description: The owner of the GitHub repositories for which the token broker can issue tokens.

Repositories.description: A dictionary of repositories (keyed by 'owner/name'), each mapping a named permission/policy to its configuration.

additionalProperties.description: A dictionary of named token policies for a repository.

GitHubTokenProfileOptions.description: Defines the conditions under which a token can be issued, and the App or pre-configured token used to issue it.

AppId.description: The ID of the GitHub App (key into GitHub:Apps) used to generate the token.

AppPermissions.description: The permissions to request for the generated installation access token, keyed by permission name. At least one permission must be specified if this property is present.

TokenId.description: The identifier of a pre-configured (e.g. PAT-based) token to use instead of a GitHub App installation token.

Branches.description: The branch name patterns for which this policy is valid. If '*' is included, it must be the only entry.

Environments.description: The deployment environment names for which this policy is valid.

Events.description: The GitHub Actions workflow trigger event names for which this policy is valid. Each entry must be exactly '*' or contain only lowercase letters (a-z), digits (0-9) and underscores (_). If '*' is included, it must be the only entry.

Tags.description: The tag name patterns for which this policy is valid.

TargetRepositories.description: Additional repositories (in 'owner/name' format) that the issued token should also grant access to. If '*' is included, it must be the only entry.

Workflows.description: The workflow file names for which this policy is valid.
